def: To shut someone in a place they cannot leave; to imprison.
To kill a person deliberately and unlawfully.
The size or shape of something; also a person's calibre or a media format.
A place where the dead are buried.
To put someone or something at risk of harm.
Almost; you could even say.
The act of singing, or a song.
Relating to business or trade.
Without any payment.
In every way; entirely.
A legal order allowing the police to arrest someone.
A wrong belief or a mistake in judgement.
In no case; definitely not.
A large keyboard instrument with strings struck by hammers.
An informal word for prison.
A deep, long state of unconsciousness.
A country ruled by a king or queen.
In the end; when everything is considered.
A two-wheeled vehicle driven by an engine.
In addition; also as a passing remark.
A remarkable event or thing that can be observed.
A vehicle for travelling in space.
Kept for a particular person; also cool and distant in manner.
A vehicle that takes sick or injured people to hospital.
In the direction behind you; in reverse.
A box in which a dead body is buried or cremated.
In a way; as it were.
Extremely impressive or attractive.
So great or shocking that it is hard to grasp.
Joined together as one.
Belonging to a time that has gone by.
To take legal action against someone in court.
Shut and fastened; also not open with one's feelings.
No longer living; dead.
Filled with fear; scared.
A large, comfortable house, often with a garden.
A place where wild animals are kept for people to see.
To return something to where it was or to its owner.
A strong fortified building from the Middle Ages.
The crime of stealing.
A serious play; also an exciting or tense situation.
A husband and wife together.
A formal statement of disagreement or a legal appeal.
To travel through or examine a place to learn about it.
A place or meeting for public discussion; also an online board.
To promise firmly that something will happen or be done.
A person or group that puts money into something to earn a return.
A remark that praises someone.
A living being, especially an animal.
Food or a lure used to attract and catch an animal.
To leave a vehicle in a place for a time.
To talk in a light, friendly way.
The unfair treatment of people because of their race.
The act of pulling back from a place or position.
An agreed level of quality or a normal requirement.
The movement of vehicles on the roads.
The special seat of a king or queen; also royal power.
The moving of people or goods from one place to another.
Kindness that eases someone's sadness or worry.
A barrier of wood or wire around a piece of land.
To pause because of doubt before acting.
To come safely through something difficult.
An old flat device once used to store computer data.
A set of political or social ideas and beliefs.
Fixed or required by a contract.
